metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (DynamoDB documents table, Elasticsearch index, S3 document storage), exposes CRUD endpoints via API Gateway, and manages document lifecycle with status tracking.

tech_stack.framework: AWS CDK v1 + Next.js 14.1 + Python 3.8 Lambda
tech_stack.architecture: Serverless (API Gateway + Lambda + DynamoDB + S3 + Elasticsearch)

findings.title: Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: All authenticated users share a single IAM role with identical API access. No action-level differentiation at the API surface.
findings.gap: Cannot restrict agent to read operations while allowing humans full CRUD access.
findings.recommendation: Configure Cognito User Pool groups and map them to separate IAM roles via Identity Pool role mapping rules. Apply method-level authorization in API Gateway.

findings.title: Identity Propagation and Delegation
findings.description: API Gateway extracts Cognito JWT but Lambda functions do not propagate caller identity to downstream service calls. No distinction between agent-as-self and agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: No identity propagation through internal service calls. Cannot distinguish agent-initiated vs human-initiated operations.
findings.recommendation: Extract sub and email claims from Cognito JWT in API processor Lambda and propagate as metadata through SQS messages and DynamoDB records.

findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: API Gateway JSON access logging configured but no CloudTrail, no immutable log storage. CloudWatch logs are not tamper-evident.
findings.gap: Logs are not immutable. No CloudTrail trail in IaC. Admin users could delete log groups.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudTrail trail to CDK stack with log file validation and S3 delivery with object lock.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, not a deployment blocker but audit trail matters for forensics.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: AUTH-Q6 resolves to RISK-SAFETY under read-only scope because audit logging is still important but not a blocker when agents are not writing data.

findings.title: Compensation and Rollback
findings.description: Document processing pipeline has no compensation or rollback logic. Failed processing leaves partial state (DynamoDB record with IN_PROGRESS, partial S3 objects).
findings.gap: No saga pattern, no compensating transactions. DLQs capture errors but do not trigger rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement cleanup Lambda triggered by DLQ that removes partial outputs when processing fails.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, compensation is relevant for system maturity but not a deployment blocker.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: STATE-Q1 resolves to RISK-SAFETY under read-only scope because agents are not executing write workflows.

findings.title: Circuit Breakers and Resilience
findings.description: Application relies on boto3 retry (max_attempts=30) with no circuit breaker pattern, no exponential backoff beyond defaults.
findings.gap: No circuit breaker to prevent cascading failures when Textract or Elasticsearch degrades.
findings.recommendation: Implement circuit breaker logic for external service calls using circuitbreaker library.

findings.title: Rate Limiting and Throttling
findings.description: Lambda concurrency (30) provides global throttle. No per-caller rate limiting. API Gateway usage plan explicitly not configured.
findings.gap: No per-caller rate limiting. Single caller can consume all capacity.
findings.recommendation: Create API Gateway Usage Plan with throttle settings and per-key tracking.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Classification
findings.description: System stores user-uploaded documents. B1 CLEAR (API returns metadata only). B2 fires: no access differentiation — all authenticated users access all documents.
findings.gap: No mechanism to restrict document access by caller identity or sensitivity level.
findings.recommendation: Add document ownership and implement access filtering in API processor Lambda.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only. B2 fires because no access differentiation exists.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q1 B1 is CLEAR (API returns only metadata). B2 fires at RISK-SAFETY because all callers have identical access with no differentiation.

b1.reasoning: API responses return document metadata only (documentId, bucketName, objectName, documentStatus) — no sensitive fields exposed.

b2.reasoning: All authenticated users have identical access. No OAuth scopes, no role differentiation, no per-user document filtering.

b3.reasoning: No formal classification metadata (no tags, no annotations, no Macie).

findings.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty
findings.description: Single-region deployment with no residency controls or documentation. User documents could contain regulated data without classification.
findings.gap: No data residency awareness. Agent reading documents and sending to cross-region LLM could violate sovereignty requirements.
findings.recommendation: Document residency requirements. Add classification at upload. Configure region-local LLM endpoints.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, data residency still relevant but lower risk than write-enabled.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q2 resolves to RISK-SAFETY under read-only scope because the agent could still transmit document data to a cross-region LLM for analysis.

findings.title: PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: Lambda functions log full request payloads and DynamoDB responses without PII filtering. Document names and metadata logged unmasked.
findings.gap: No PII masking or log scrubbing in any Lambda function.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with PII filtering using aws-lambda-powertools.

findings.title: Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy specification files exist. API defined only in CDK code.
findings.gap: No machine-readable spec for automated agent tool generation.
findings.recommendation: Export OpenAPI spec from deployed API Gateway or generate from CDK annotations.

findings.title: Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses use unstructured strings with HTTP 200 status regardless of error. No error codes or retryable indicators.
findings.gap: Agent cannot programmatically distinguish error types or determine retryability.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured error format with proper HTTP status codes and error taxonomy.

findings.title: Sandbox/Staging Environment
findings.description: No separate staging or sandbox environment. Single deployment stack. No local testing setup.
findings.gap: No safe environment for agent testing before production.
findings.recommendation: Create a staging CDK context that deploys a separate stack with reduced capacity.

findings.title: Temporal Metadata and Freshness
findings.description: Documents have UTC timestamps (documentCreatedOn, documentCompletedOn) but no freshness signaling in API responses.
findings.gap: No Cache-Control or freshness indicators in API responses.
findings.recommendation: Add Last-Modified and Cache-Control response headers.

findings.title: Schema Versioning and API Contracts
findings.description: No API versioning, no schema files, no breaking change detection in CI.
findings.gap: Agent tool schemas can break silently when API changes.
findings.recommendation: Add API versioning and breaking change detection in CI pipeline.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging
findings.description: X-Ray tracing ACTIVE on all Lambdas. API Gateway JSON access logs. But Lambda logs use unstructured print() with no correlation ID.
findings.gap: Cannot correlate API Gateway requests with Lambda logs without X-Ray trace lookup.
findings.recommendation: Replace print() with aws-lambda-powertools structured logging with trace correlation.

findings.title: Alerting on Error Rates and Latency
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms configured. No anomaly detection. Passive monitoring only.
findings.gap: No automated alerting for agent-impacting degradation.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for API Gateway error rates, Lambda errors, DLQ depth, and P99 latency.

findings.title: Infrastructure Governance
findings.description: CDK IaC exists. CodePipeline CI/CD exists. No drift detection. No mandatory peer review before deployment.
findings.gap: Missing drift detection and review gates for infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: Add approval stage in CodePipeline and AWS Config drift detection rules.

findings.title: CI/CD with API Contract Testing
findings.description: CodePipeline exists but runs minimal tests. No API contract testing or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: API breaking changes not caught before deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add API integration tests and OpenAPI diff in CI pipeline.

findings.title: Rollback Capability
findings.description: No rollback mechanism configured. In-place CDK deployment. Recovery requires new pipeline execution.
findings.gap: Cannot quickly roll back broken deployments affecting agent-facing APIs.
findings.recommendation: Add deployment monitoring and automatic rollback triggers.

findings.title: API Test Coverage
findings.description: Minimal test coverage: 2 Python unit tests. No API tests, no integration tests, no contract tests.
findings.gap: Agent-facing API endpoints have no automated test coverage.
findings.recommendation: Add comprehensive API test suite using pytest with moto for AWS service mocking.

evaluations.title: Documented API Interface
evaluations.reason: REST API exists via API Gateway with documented routes.

evaluations.title: Idempotent Write Operations
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — no finding emitted as INFO is architectural guidance only for this conditional question.

evaluations.title: Structured Response Format
evaluations.reason: JSON responses — no concern for agent consumption.

evaluations.title: Asynchronous Operation Support
evaluations.reason: Async patterns exist (SQS, DDB Streams, status polling).

evaluations.title: Event Emission for State Changes
evaluations.reason: INFO finding — internal events exist but no external webhooks. Architectural guidance.

evaluations.title: Rate Limit Documentation and Headers
evaluations.reason: INFO finding — no rate limit headers. Architectural guidance.

evaluations.title: Machine Identity Authentication
evaluations.reason: Cognito User Pool + API Gateway COGNITO_USER_POOLS authorizer supports machine identity via programmatic user creation.

evaluations.title: Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege)
evaluations.reason: Per-Lambda IAM roles demonstrate scope differentiation.

evaluations.title: Credential Management
evaluations.reason: IAM role-based authentication — no static credentials in code.

evaluations.title: Agent Identity Suspension
evaluations.reason: Cognito AdminDisableUser API enables immediate identity suspension.

evaluations.title: Queryable Current State
evaluations.reason: GET /documents and GET /document expose current state with status field.

evaluations.title: Concurrency Controls
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope.

evaluations.title: Blast Radius and Transaction Limits
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope.

evaluations.title: Graceful Degradation Signaling
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — no P0 priority set and not on critical path.

evaluations.title: Draft/Pending State
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope.

evaluations.title: Configurable Approval Gates
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope.

evaluations.title: Selective Query Support
evaluations.reason: Pagination (pageSize=25, nextToken) and search filtering exist.

evaluations.title: Input Validation and Schema Enforcement
evaluations.reason: Systematic input validation exists at API boundary.

evaluations.title: Data Quality Awareness
evaluations.reason: INFO finding — no quality metrics. Architectural guidance.

evaluations.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
evaluations.reason: Field names are human-readable (documentId, bucketName, objectName, documentStatus).

evaluations.title: Data Catalog / Metadata Layer
evaluations.reason: INFO finding — no data catalog. Architectural guidance.

evaluations.title: Business Outcome Metrics
evaluations.reason: INFO finding — no custom business metrics. Architectural guidance.

evaluations.title: Encryption at Rest
evaluations.reason: All data stores encrypted: S3 (S3_MANAGED), DynamoDB (SSE), Elasticsearch (KMS), SQS (KMS).

items.summary: Address RISK-SAFETY findings to enable supervised pilot. Focus on access differentiation, audit logging, rate limiting, and PII protection.
Address RISK-QUALITY findings to improve agent effectiveness. Focus on API documentation, observability, and deployment maturity.
Address remaining quality findings. Expand test coverage for agent-facing APIs.

recommended_actions.action: Implement per-caller access control and rate limiting
recommended_actions.rationale: These three findings share a root cause: all authenticated callers are treated identically. Implementing Cognito groups with role-based access solves action-level auth, data access differentiation, and per-caller rate limiting together.

recommended_actions.action: Deploy immutable audit logging with CloudTrail
recommended_actions.rationale: CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 object lock provides tamper-evident audit trail for agent actions.

recommended_actions.action: Add circuit breakers and structured logging
recommended_actions.rationale: Adopting aws-lambda-powertools addresses structured logging, PII filtering, and provides patterns for circuit breaker implementation.
An OpenAPI spec enables automated agent tool generation and provides the foundation for contract testing and versioning.

recommended_actions.action: Establish staging environment and expand test coverage
recommended_actions.rationale: A staging environment enables safe agent testing. API tests validate agent-facing contract stability.
